data.content:
### POST /logout

Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md

Logs out the currently authenticated user by revoking all their refresh tokens. JWT access tokens remain valid until they expire.

```APIDOC
## POST /logout

### Description
Logs out the currently authenticated user by revoking all their refresh tokens.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/logout

### Parameters
Requires authentication via the `Authorization` header.

### Response
(Typically returns a success status or empty body on successful logout.)
```

--------------------------------

### Get Token - Password/Phone Login

Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md

This endpoint retrieves an access token using either password or phone login. The request requires query parameters like `grant_type` and a body containing either email/password or phone/password credentials.  It returns a JSON object containing an access token, token type, expiration time, and a refresh token.

```javascript
// Email login
{
  "email": "name@domain.com",
  "password": "somepassword"
}

// Phone login
{
  "phone": "12345678",
  "password": "somepassword"
}
```

```json
{
  "refresh_token": "a-refresh-token"
}
```

--------------------------------

### POST /token

Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md

Authenticates users using either the password grant type (email/phone and password) or the refresh token grant type. Used to obtain JWT tokens for accessing protected resources.

```APIDOC
## POST /token

### Description
Obtains JWT access and refresh tokens using password or refresh token grant types.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/token

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **grant_type** (string) - Required - Specifies the grant type. Accepts 'password' or 'refresh_token'.

#### Request Body

**For `grant_type=password`:**
- **email** (string) - Required (if not using phone) - The user's email address.
- **phone** (string) - Required (if not using email) - The user's phone number.
- **password** (string) - Required - The user's password.

**For `grant_type=refresh_token`:**
- **refresh_token** (string) - Required - The user's refresh token.

### Request Example (Password Grant)
```json
{
  "email": "name@domain.com",
  "password": "somepassword"
}
```

### Request Example (Refresh Token Grant)
```json
{
  "refresh_token": "a-refresh-token"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **access_token** (string) - JWT token for accessing protected resources.
- **token_type** (string) - Type of the token, typically 'bearer'.
- **expires_in** (integer) - Expiration time of the access token in seconds.
- **refresh_token** (string) - Token used to obtain a new access token.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "access_token": "jwt-token-representing-the-user",
  "token_type": "bearer",
  "expires_in": 3600,
  "refresh_token": "a-refresh-token"
}
```

**Usage**: Use the `Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN_HERE` header for authenticated requests.
```

--------------------------------

### GET /callback

Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md

The callback endpoint for external OAuth providers. Handles the redirection after user authentication with an external provider and extracts authentication tokens.

```APIDOC
## GET /callback

### Description
This endpoint is called by external OAuth providers after the user has successfully authenticated.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/callback

### Parameters
This endpoint receives parameters from the OAuth provider as query parameters.

### Response
Redirects the user to `<GOTRUE_SITE_URL>` with authentication tokens in the URL fragment:
`#access_token=<access_token>&refresh_token=<refresh_token>&provider_token=<provider_oauth_token>&expires_in=3600&provider=<provider_name>`

**Note**: The `provider_token` may contain additional OAuth tokens from the provider, useful for fetching further user data or interacting with provider services.
```

--------------------------------

### Reauthenticate - Headers

Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md

This endpoint sends a nonce to the user's email or phone, requiring the user to be logged in/authenticated. Requires an Authorization header with a bearer token. This endpoint requires the user to have either an email or phone number.

```javascript
headers: {
  "Authorization" : "Bearer eyJhbGciOiJI...M3A90LCkxxtX9oNP9KZO"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Send Magic Link - JSON

Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md

This endpoint delivers a magic link to the user's email address, allowing them to redeem an access token. It's recommended to use /otp instead for improved security. The input is a JSON object containing the user's email address. The endpoint returns an empty response.

```json
{
  "email": "email@example.com"
}
```

--------------------------------

### POST /magiclink

Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md

Sends a magic link to the user's email address, which can be used to redeem an access token. Note: It's recommended to use `/otp` instead. Rate limited to once every 60 seconds.

```APIDOC
## POST /magiclink

### Description
Sends a magic link to the user's email address for authentication.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/magiclink

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **email** (string) - Required - The email address of the user.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "email": "email@example.com"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
Returns an empty JSON object upon successful request.

#### Response Example
```json
{}
```

**Note**: The magic link, when clicked, redirects to `<SITE_URL>#access_token=x&refresh_token=y&expires_in=z&token_type=bearer&type=magiclink`.
```

--------------------------------

### POST /admin/generate_link

Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md

This endpoint generates an email action link based on the specified type, such as signup, magiclink, recovery, or invite. It requires admin authorization and includes details like the action link, email OTP, hashed token, verification type, and redirect URL. The response provides query parameters separately for convenience along with the email OTP and hashed token.

```APIDOC
## POST /admin/generate_link

### Description
Generates an email action link based on the type (signup, magiclink, recovery, or invite) and returns the link along with query parameters, email OTP, hashed token, and verification details. Requires admin authorization.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/admin/generate_link

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
- **type** (string) - Required - The type of link to generate: 'signup', 'magiclink', 'recovery', or 'invite'.
- **email** (string) - Required - The email address for the link.
- **password** (string) - Optional - User's password, required if type is 'signup'.
- **data** (object) - Optional - Additional signup data, required if type is 'signup'.
- **redirect_to** (string) - Optional - Redirect URL after email action, defaults to SITE_URL.

### Request Example
{
  "type": "signup",
  "email": "email@example.com",
  "password": "secret",
  "data": {
    ...
  },
  "redirect_to": "https://supabase.io"
}

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **action_link** (string) - The full action link URL.
- **email_otp** (string) - The email OTP code.
- **hashed_token** (string) - The hashed token.
- **verification_type** (string) - The type of verification.
- **redirect_to** (string) - The redirect URL after action.

#### Response Example
{
  "action_link": "http://localhost:9999/verify?token=TOKEN&type=TYPE&redirect_to=REDIRECT_URL",
  "email_otp": "EMAIL_OTP",
  "hashed_token": "TOKEN",
  "verification_type": "TYPE",
  "redirect_to": "REDIRECT_URL",
  ...
}
```

--------------------------------

### HTTPS Server Setup for Apple OAuth

Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md

Go function that implements HTTPS server setup for Apple OAuth authentication. Replaces the standard HTTP ListenAndServe with TLS-enabled version using certificate and key files. Includes proper shutdown handling and context timeout management.

```go
func (a *API) ListenAndServe(hostAndPort string) {
  log := logrus.WithField("component", "api")
  path, err := os.Getwd()
  if err != nil {
    log.Println(err)
  }
  server := &http.Server{
    Addr:    hostAndPort,
    Handler: a.handler,
  }
  done := make(chan struct{})
  defer close(done)
  go func() {
    waitForTermination(log, done)
    ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), time.Minute)
    defer cancel()
    server.Shutdown(ctx)
  }()
  if err := server.ListenAndServeTLS("PATH_TO_CRT_FILE", "PATH_TO_KEY_FILE"); err != http.ErrServerClosed {
    log.WithError(err).Fatal("http server listen failed")
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### GET /user

Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md

Retrieves the JSON object for the currently logged-in user. Requires authentication.

```APIDOC
## GET /user

### Description
Retrieves the user profile information for the currently authenticated user.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/user

### Parameters
Requires authentication via the `Authorization` header.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **id** (string) - The unique identifier of the user.
- **email** (string) - The email address of the user.
- **confirmation_sent_at** (string) - Timestamp when the confirmation email was sent.
- **created_at** (string) - Timestamp when the user account was created.
- **updated_at** (string) - Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "id": "11111111-2222-3333-4444-5555555555555",
  "email": "email@example.com",
  "confirmation_sent_at": "2016-05-15T20:49:40.882805774-07:00",
  "created_at": "2016-05-15T19:53:12.368652374-07:00",
  "updated_at": "2016-05-15T19:53:12.368652374-07:00"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### GET /verify

Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md

Verify a registration or password recovery via query params. User will be logged in and redirected.

```APIDOC
## GET /verify

### Description
Verify a registration or a password recovery via query params. User will be logged in and redirected.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/verify

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **type** (string) - Required - Type of verification (signup, recovery, magiclink, invite)
- **token** (string) - Required - Token from /signup, /recover, or /magiclink
- **redirect_to** (string) - Optional - URL to redirect after verification

### Request Example
```json
{
  "type": "signup",
  "token": "confirmation-code-delivered-in-email",
  "redirect_to": "https://supabase.io"
}
```

### Response
User will be redirected to:
```
SITE_URL/#access_token=jwt-token-representing-the-user&token_type=bearer&expires_in=3600&refresh_token=a-refresh-token&type=invite
```
```

--------------------------------

### Get User - JSON

Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md

This endpoint retrieves the JSON object for the logged-in user. It requires authentication. The endpoint returns a JSON object containing user information, including ID, email, creation/update timestamps.

```json
{
  "id": "11111111-2222-3333-4444-5555555555555",
  "email": "email@example.com",
  "confirmation_sent_at": "2016-05-15T20:49:40.882805774-07:00",
  "created_at": "2016-05-15T19:53:12.368652374-07:00",
  "updated_at": "2016-05-15T19:53:12.368652374-07:00"
}
```

--------------------------------

### POST /resend

Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md

Resend an existing signup, SMS, email_change, or phone_change OTP.

```APIDOC
## POST /resend

### Description
Allows a user to resend an existing signup, SMS, email_change, or phone_change OTP.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/resend

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **email** (string) - Optional - User's email address
- **phone** (string) - Optional - User's phone number in E.164 format
- **type** (string) - Required - Type of OTP to resend (signup, sms, email_change, phone_change)

### Request Example
```json
{
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "type": "signup"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **message_id** (string) - ID of the sent message

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "message_id": "msgid123456"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### GET /reauthenticate

Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md

Sends a nonce to the user's registered email or phone number for reauthentication. This endpoint requires the user to be already logged in.

```APIDOC
## GET /reauthenticate

### Description
Sends a nonce to the user's verified email or phone number to reauthenticate them. Requires the user to be currently logged in.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/reauthenticate

### Parameters
Requires authentication via the `Authorization` header.

### Request Example
```js
headers: {
  "Authorization" : "Bearer eyJhbGciOiJI...M3A90LCkxxtX9oNP9KZO"
}
```

### Response
(Details of the response body are not provided in the source text, but typically indicates success or failure of sending the nonce.)
```
